function_reachability: Is it impossible for function 'printf' to reach function 'main'?
dominators: Is it guaranteed that whenever line 3:  <statement> printf("This computing enviroments uses:\n"); </statement> is reached, the statement line 9:  <statement> printf("%d bytes for floats\n",sizeof(float)); </statement> has already been reached?

Is it guaranteed that whenever line 11:  <statement> printf("%d bytes for pointers\n",sizeof(int *)); </statement> is reached, the statement line 8:  <statement> printf("%d bytes for long longs\n",sizeof(long long int)); </statement> has already been reached?
dead_code: Is the statement 'printf("%d bytes for long longs\n",sizeof(long long int));' unreachable?
Is this statement not dead: “printf("%d bytes for ints\n",sizeof(int));”?
